entry.title: NY Times: 30 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Biden Savors Much-Needed Victories. But Will the Highs Overshadow the Lows?
  • How Joe Manchin Shaped the Climate Change Bill
  • Kentucky: Flooding Leaves 25 Dead as Search for Victims Continues
  • Family Loses Four Children in Kentucky Flood Waters
  • Europe’s Race to Secure New Energy Sources Is on a Knife’s Edge
  • US Defense Department Official Says Russia’s War Effort Is Failing
  • As Inventory Piles Up, Liquidation Warehouses Are Busy
  • A Polish Priest’s War Against Abortion Focuses on Helping Single Mothers
  • Could the G.O.P. Beat Whitmer in Michigan? Party Chaos Has Hurt Its Chances.
  • Fox News, Once Home to Trump, Now Often Ignores Him
  • How to Get a Monkeypox Vaccine in N.Y.C.
  • How Did a Two-Time Killer Get Out to Be Charged Again at Age 83?
  • Brittney Griner and the Total Lopsidedness of Prisoner Swaps with Russia
  • Women, the Game Is Rigged. It’s Time We Stop Playing by the Rules.
  • Why Andrew Yang’s New Third Party Is Bound to Fail
  • How Do Workers’ Wages Relate to Inflation?
  • Podcast Recommendations
  • Gangs Advance on the Seat of Haitian Government Power: ‘Haitians Are Hostages’
  • Should You Worry About Debris From China’s Big Rocket Booster?
  • Heavy Rain Causes Deadly Flooding Across Iran

  • Francis Calls Abuse of Indigenous People in Canada a ‘Genocide’
  • Live Updates: Ukraine Seeks Inquiry Into Killing of Dozens at Russian Prison Camp
  • Germans Tiptoe Up the Path to Energy Savings
  • Dr. Oh Eun-young of ‘My Golden Kids’ Puts Therapy on TV
  • Pressure Grows for U.A.E. to Free Asim Ghafoor, American Lawyer
  • ‘Wagatha Christie’ Trial: Judge Finds No Libel
  • Why Pelosi’s Proposed Taiwan Visit Is Raising U.S.-China Tensions
  • Excitement, Then Ambivalence, For the Pope’s Visit to Quebec
  • Vessels await word that they can carry the first grain shipments out of Ukraine.
  • Breaking Agreement to Use a Condom Is a Sex Crime, Canada High Court Rules
  • Blinken and Lavrov Discuss Griner in Their First Call of the War
  • Explosion Kills Dozens of Ukrainian Captives at Russian-Held Prison
  • A Ukrainian appeals court reduces the life sentence of a Russian soldier tried for war crimes.
  • Three Pressing Questions About Monkeypox: Spread, Vaccination, Treatment
  • Blinken and Lavrov Discuss Prisoner Swap Involving Griner and Whelan
  • Lebanon Weighs Ukraine Claim That Syrian Ship Holds Stolen Grain
  • I’ll Trade You Some Cursed Baby Dolls and Metal Frogs for That Unwanted Banana Statue
  • Facing Gas Shortages, Europe Seeks to Shrink Demand for Energy
  • Hong Kong: Mirror Concert Accident to Be Investigated
  • Russian National Charged With Spreading Propaganda Through U.S. Groups
  • The Country That Wants to ‘Be Average’ vs. Jeff Bezos and His $500 Million Yacht
  • At the Parkland Trial, Families Must Endure Grisly Evidence on Repeat
  • Trump Backs Tudor Dixon in Michigan’s Chaotic G.O.P. Governor Primary
  • Kate Bedingfield, White House Communications Director, Decides to Stay at White House
  • Death Toll Rises in Devastating Kentucky Flooding
  • Democrats’ Plan to Win in 2022 Looks a Lot Like 2020 and 2018
  • House Passes Assault Weapons Ban That Is Doomed in Senate
  • Blinken Resists Push to Label Russia a Terrorist State
  • Video: Las Vegas Valley Flooded by Monsoon Rains
  • Winning Mega Millions Ticket Sold in Illinois for $1.28 Billion Jackpot
  • Here’s How to Help Victims of the Flooding in Kentucky
  • Pushing an Immigration Conspiracy Theory, While Courting Latinos
  • St. Louis Floods Again After Second Round of Storms
  • Son Pulls His Mother to Safety in Kentucky Floods
  • Despite Biden’s Success on Judges, Progressives Demand Faster Pace
  • If the Economy Is Shaky, Why Are Company Profits Still Strong?
  • Climate Bill ‘Transformative’ for Auto and Energy Industries
  • Pay growth and prices picked up, keeping the Fed on track for rate increases.
  • How to Cope With Rising Rates and Inflation
  • How to Adjust Your Stock Strategy in a Volatile Market
  • The S&P 500 had its best month since November 2020.
  • Why Don’t Many Hotels Install Carbon Monoxide Alarms?
  • Fed’s Kashkari says officials are ‘a long way’ from backing off inflation fight.
  • The One Thing You Can Control at Work Is You
  • Antenna Group Emerges as Bidder for Vice Media
  • Allergan Reaches Tentative $2.37 Billion Deal to Settle Opioid Suits
  • Large landlords aggressively moved against renters in the pandemic, a report says.
  • Oil Company Buybacks Surge Along With Record Profits
  • Exxon and Chevron Report Record Profits on High Oil Prices
  • Eurozone Economy Grew Faster Than Expected, but So Did Prices
  • Does Twitch Fame Have to Come With a Stalker?
  • Help! My Lost Luggage Wasn’t Delivered for 12 Days. I Want Restitution.
  • The Default Tech Settings You Should Turn Off Right Away
News from the New York Times

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 30 Jul, 2022

entry.content:

entry.title: Guardian: 30 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • Tour de France Femmes: riders head into the mountains on stage seven – live!

  • Brisbane teenager built spyware used by domestic violence perpetrators across world, police allege
  • Deliverance at 50: a violent battle between urban and rural America
  • Blind date: ‘Did we kiss? A lady never kisses and tells …’
  • Theme parks were not meant for Black families: why racism at Sesame Place is part of a shameful tradition
  • ‘People are turning off’: Muscovites put the war aside and enjoy summer

entry.content:

  • Trans people shouldn’t have to hide to help Democrats win

  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 30 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 30 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-30 14:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • Ukraine war: US envoy to UN says Russia intends to dissolve Ukraine from world map

  • US House passes assault rifle ban, but bill likely doomed to fail in Senate
  • Pope visits remote territory on the edge of the Arctic for final apology of his Canadian tour
  • Elon Musk files countersuit under seal vs Twitter over US$44 billion deal
  • Russian operative charged with using US groups to spread propaganda
  • Spain and Brazil report first monkeypox-related deaths outside Africa

  • Will Smith posts apology video for slapping Chris Rock at Oscars ceremony in March
  • China, slowly but surely, is waking up and smelling the coffee
  • Cannibalism with casual couplings, human body parts turned into furniture – madness is the new normal in Sayaka Murata’s short stories
  • 7 new K-dramas to watch in August 2022: Netflix’s A Model Family, If You Wish Upon Me, The Law Cafe, and more
  • Why Stranger Things’ Sadie Sink kills it on the red carpet – she embraces Chanel, Gucci, Prada and looks stunning in a blazer
  • Museum of Art and Photography in Bangalore, India, opening soon, will tell the story of Indian art in an entertaining way, founder says
  • On Malaysia’s toughest trek, through Malayan tiger’s last refuge, the rainforest of Tamara Negara national park, a sweaty climb and muddy descent
  • Sandalwood: its history, medicinal uses, Buddhist connections, arrival in China, and how the trade flourished in Asia
  • Get on your bike and ride: top 5 ultra-cycling races for amateurs – from mountains of Kyrgyzstan to wilds of South Africa
  • What is your psychological age? Free online test from Hong Kong-based longevity firm gives answers and tips
  • Who will get to own the newly discovered Lulo Rose diamond? The 170-carat pink gem is such a rare find, the likes of Louis Vuitton or Graff could snap it up for US$100 million at auction
  • 12 richest NBA players of 2022 – net worths, ranked: from billionaires LeBron James and Michael Jordan and their Nike deals, to Shaquille O’Neal and Magic Johnson – but who became a Coca-Cola CEO?
  • Who was Colonel Tom Parker, Elvis Presley’s manager who allegedly stole over half of his millions? Inside their controversial connection, depicted in Baz Luhrmann’s new biopic
  • Where is Hunter Moore’s ex-girlfriend Kirra Hughes now? The Most Hated Man on the Internet docu showed her side of the story – now she’s modelling, Instagramming and living with a new love
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 30 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ukraine, NPT review confab dominate peace symposium
  • Over 30,000 COVID cases in Tokyo; Okinawa sets record
  • Governors get free hand to take stronger steps against BA.5

  • With so many COVID cases, daily counts now meaningless
  • Japan’s Hayashi says ‘logic of brute force’ gaining traction in Indo-Pacific

  • Disneyland eyes more complex ticketing to level visitor numbers
  • China's Xi warns Biden over Taiwan, calls for cooperation
  • For Taiwan, Pelosi visit is about U.S., China controlling risk
  • China accuses U.S. of ‘navigation bullying’ in South China Sea
  • N. Korea claims no new fever cases amid doubts over COVID-19 data
  • Stargazers say traces of June 29 fireball likely fell across Tokyo
  • EDITORIAL: Ishin has duty to respond to local concerns about casino project
  • VOX POPULI: Was gift to Japan from ancient China a rush job after all?
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.
